checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only the text present in the source PDF. All paragraphs in the body of the letter and the header information correspond directly to the source document content across both pages. No fabricated sections or sentences were detected.

All contact information was verified against the source PDF. The mailing address (2 S. Salisbury Street, Raleigh, NC 27699-0601), phone number ((919) 807-7500), fax number ((919) 807-7647), and the URL (www.ncauditor.net) are identical to the source. The addition of the 'https://' protocol to the hyperlink in the output body is a standard accessibility/functionality enhancement and does not constitute a fabrication of the address itself.

The output successfully captures the content of both page 1 and page 2 of the source document. All body paragraphs, the letterhead info, recipient details, and the closing/CC information are present in the semantic output.
The source document contains no tables; therefore, no table rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section of the document has been fully converted. Comparing the word counts and paragraph structure reveals that no significant text content was omitted from the letter's body or the supporting administrative text.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output JSON preserves the exact wording, dates, and amounts found in the source document. For example, fiscal grant amounts ($8,619 and $10,496) and dates (April 14, 2008 and March 28, 2008) are precisely maintained. Punctuation and capitalisation match the original intent of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The document was reviewed for duplicated content blocks. The name and title of the State Auditor appear twice in the output, but this accurately reflects the source document where it is present in both the sidebar header on page 1 and the signature block on page 2. No other paragraphs or sections are repeated.

The output contains no heading elements, treating all text as paragraphs. While the main title 'STATE OF NORTH CAROLINA Office of the State Auditor' could have been an H1, the use of a flat structure for a formal letter is a permissible normalization and does not constitute a skip or broken hierarchy as there are no levels to skip.
The source document contains no tables.

The output follows the logical reading order of the source document. The header elements are linearized reasonably (Seal -> Department Title -> Auditor Name -> Sidebar info), and the body text maintains the correct sequence from the first page into the second page signature block. The omission of the page 2 header is not a reading order defect.

There are no formal bulleted or numbered lists in the source document. Text elements that serve list-like functions (such as the 'RE:' line) are correctly preserved through newline formatting within paragraphs.
The URLs clearly visible in the source document (www.ncauditor.net) are correctly captured as functional links in the output. No extraneous or fabricated links were introduced.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the visual organization and logical flow of the source document. The conversion from a two-page PDF layout to a single-column scrolling HTML layout is handled correctly. The sequence of information—from the letterhead to the recipient details, body paragraphs, and closing—matches the source. Differences in font scaling and the central placement of the seal are acceptable responsive layout adjustments.
There are no formal tables in the source document. The itemized financial figures in the 'RE:' section are correctly rendered as text lines, maintaining the visual intent of the original list.

All meaningful images from the source are present in the HTML output. This includes the Seal of the State of North Carolina at the top and the handwritten signature image in the closing section. Both images are clear and functional.

A thorough visual inspection of the rendered HTML confirms that there is no duplicated body content. Each paragraph and section from the original two pages is rendered exactly once. The document title added at the top is a standard conversion feature and not a duplication of existing content.
